channel.title: Comments on: Southern Tier Creme Brulee Imperial Milk Stout – Ep. 99
channel.description: Beer America TV is the first beer web site dedicated to the specialty craft brewing world with video on industry experts and insiders plus more!

item.title: By: Paul
item.dc:creator: Paul
item.description:
Thanks Bill and Marco!
Bill, I don't think we're going to make it to NY this year unfortunately. I moved to Fort Worth Texas and usually spend a few weeks every summer back in NY state, but this year is just to busy. I'll let you know if I do however, it would be nice to meet you too!
item.content:encoded:
Thanks Bill and Marco!

Bill, I don’t think we’re going to make it to NY this year unfortunately. I moved to Fort Worth Texas and usually spend a few weeks every summer back in NY state, but this year is just to busy. I’ll let you know if I do however, it would be nice to meet you too!

item.title: By: Bill Davis
item.dc:creator: Bill Davis
item.description:
Hey Guys,
Its always good to see you tasting beers from my neck of the woods (Corning, NY). Have you guys had the opportunity to try anything from Horseheads Brewing? They have a very nice variety and probably one of the best seasonal pumpkin beers I have had the pleasure to sample.
http://www.horseheadsbrewing.com/
p.s. Are either of you guys going to make it to any of the NY beer festivals this summer? If so I would love to meet you in person.
item.content:encoded:
Hey Guys,

Its always good to see you tasting beers from my neck of the woods (Corning, NY). Have you guys had the opportunity to try anything from Horseheads Brewing? They have a very nice variety and probably one of the best seasonal pumpkin beers I have had the pleasure to sample.

http://www.horseheadsbrewing.com/

p.s. Are either of you guys going to make it to any of the NY beer festivals this summer? If so I would love to meet you in person.

item.title: By: Marco
item.dc:creator: Marco
item.description: Good show guys! Sounds like an interesting beer.
item.content:encoded: Good show guys! Sounds like an interesting beer.
